title: Physical Media Strikes Back With Tim Simons
format: Sean and actor Tim Simons pick up their disc-collecting conversation 14 months later: Best Buy pulling the racks, whether a director gets to recut a movie after it's out, and the repertory titles worth chasing on 4K.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: Its 4K sold out and made the trade press; Sean also hosted a live IMAX screening with the cinematographer, editor, and composer.

films.title: True Romance
films.note: Both own it on DVD and Blu-ray; Sean weighs whether the $55 Arrow 4K is worth a re-buy of a movie he'd just stream anyway.

films.title: Waterworld
films.note: Sean bought the 4K to prep a planned Kevin Costner episode, granting it 'movie history' value even if it's not good.

films.title: Mallrats
films.note: The line-item both did the math on and passed: loved it at 17, doesn't watch it now, and the Affleck character 'is comically canceled.'

films.title: The Last of the Mohicans
films.note: Tim's recent Michael Mann completion buy.

films.title: Ferrari
films.note: On pre-order: 'it feels fun to hit a pre-order button.'

films.title: Cloak & Dagger
films.note: A childhood favorite Tim picked up in Arrow's box set.

films.title: Showgirls
films.note: Tim owns the Arrow box; one of the emotionally-attached titles he'd consider cycling out.

films.title: RoboCop
films.note: Another Arrow release Tim holds onto for the attachment, not the art.

films.title: First Reformed
films.note: Tim found it used at CD Trader in Tarzana for $5.99; a movie that spiked way up for him.

films.title: Mean Streets
films.note: A $4 pawn-shop find on a family vacation in Maine.

films.title: The Way of the Gun
films.note: Sean's example of the McQuarrie disc he'd never give away in a listener contest.

films.title: Joe
films.note: The blind pick that kicked off Tim's viewing project, maybe the best movie he saw this year.

films.title: Hardcore
films.note: Schrader/George C. Scott; on Tim's list because Tarantino wrote about it in 'Cinema Speculation.'

films.title: Rolling Thunder
films.note: A borderline-exploitation veteran-revenge movie Sean links, straight-faced, to Con Air.

films.title: Con Air
films.note: Sean's bit: a disrespected veteran who goes to prison and needs vindication and reunion, 'they're the same movie' as Rolling Thunder.

films.title: The Devils
films.note: Ken Russell's banned church horror; got the project back on the grimy track, and Tim paused it early to ask if it was the best movie he'd ever seen.

films.title: Possession
films.note: The Zulawski freakout they watched next; only on Shudder, so Tim wants a disc of it.

films.title: The Third Part of the Night
films.note: In the Eureka Zulawski box Sean bought on sale.

films.title: The Devil
films.note: Also in the Zulawski box.

films.title: On the Silver Globe
films.note: The box's centerpiece: 'one of the craziest science fiction movies ever made.'

films.title: Big Night
films.note: The 'hit bottom' pick: a great movie, but too tasteful for the grimy run they were on.

films.title: Hearts and Minds
films.note: Where editor Lindsay Clingman (subject of Tim's through-the-editor project) started.

films.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
films.note: Clingman's next credit after 'Hearts and Minds.'

films.title: Ali
films.note: Clingman was fired off it by Michael Mann.

films.title: Hair
films.note: Another Clingman edit Tim worked through.

films.title: Don't Look Back
films.note: Shot by Richard Pearce, Clingman's collaborator.

films.title: Woodstock
films.note: Also in Pearce's camera credits.

segments.heading: Recuts, restorations, and lossless sound
segments.note: who owns the movie after it's out

films.title: Miller's Crossing
films.note: The Criterion 4K's very green grade is where Sean first noticed color 'corrections' that don't look like the movie he grew up on.

films.title: The Raid
films.note: The new recolored 4K, cited as part of the transfer controversy.

films.title: True Lies
films.note: Among the James Cameron 4Ks people are frustrated by.

films.title: The Abyss
films.note: Looked great on film, disappointing on the 4K disc.

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: Sean burned the fan 'de-specialized' original-color trilogy to Blu-ray, 'because I'm a fucking psychopath.'

films.title: Wings of Desire
films.note: Wim Wenders told Sean the negative was 'all wrong' and the restoration is what he always intended. Sean isn't sure he wants to see it.

films.title: Blackhat
films.note: The Arrow 4K moves a key sequence earlier and plays more coherently; Sean thinks the movie makes no sense, Tim insists he's '100% wrong.'

films.title: Miami Vice
films.note: Sean loves the theatrical cut for throwing you into the club with no setup; Mann later added earlier scenes.

films.title: Sorcerer
films.note: Sean saw it four or five times this year, once on a red-shifted print at the New Beverly.

films.title: Kingdom of Heaven
films.note: The four-hour cut, cited as the model for the coming era of extended editions.

films.title: Dude, Where's My Car?
films.note: Sean's joke about the inevitable maximalist long cut of everything.

films.title: Bros
films.note: The 'Apatow expansion' of extra dick jokes as a preview of endless special editions.

films.title: The Power of the Dog
films.note: Went to Criterion immediately; Sean wonders what its edition looks like in 20 years.

films.title: Napoleon
films.note: The counterexample: whenever the four-and-a-half-hour cut lands, Sean will happily watch the whole thing.

films.title: Maestro
films.note: Seeing it in a theater is where Sean started to understand how a score works and matters.

films.title: Skinamarink
films.note: Terrifying alone on a laptop, silly in a chatty theater: Sean's case that format and setting change the movie.

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.note: The lossless-sound argument: don't watch it on TV speakers off a streaming service.

segments.heading: Goals, great buys, and family movie night

films.title: Messiah of Evil
films.note: Sean's favorite recent buy: Radiance Films' 4K hardbox of a hard-to-see cult horror movie.

films.title: Miami Blues
films.note: Bought at Fopp in London, first exposure to the Radiance label.

films.title: Thieves Like Us
films.note: Radiance's edition of the 'lost' Altman, the stuff Sean's always been looking for.

films.title: American Graffiti
films.note: Screenwriting credit for Messiah of Evil's Willard Huyck and Gloria Katz.

films.title: Howard the Duck
films.note: Huyck and Katz's best-known and much-maligned credit.

films.title: Women in Love
films.note: The one Ken Russell film in the Criterion Collection: Tim eyeing a Russell deep dive.

films.title: Mahler
films.note: One of Russell's operatic composer biopics; Sean calls it a Tar precursor.

films.title: The Boy Friend
films.note: A Russell title you can stream right now.

films.title: Altered States
films.note: Another Russell film in a different genre, same 'Russellian insanity.'

films.title: Tár
films.note: Invoked via the Mahler-in-the-church gag.

films.title: May December
films.note: What clicked Todd Haynes into place for Tim: his director-of-the-year deep dive.

films.title: Safe
films.note: One of the Haynes bookends Tim's already seen.

films.title: Far from Heaven
films.note: On Kino; part of the Haynes catalog to work through.

films.title: The Velvet Underground
films.note: Haynes' documentary, the one Apple film that got a physical release.

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: Sean re-watched it on Apple TV, wants to own it, and notes Apple doesn't press its films, hoping for an eventual Criterion partnership.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: Netflix film licensed to a boutique edition, per Sean.

films.title: Okja
films.note: Another streamer title available on disc.

films.title: Sound of Metal
films.note: The Amazon movie that got a Criterion edition.

films.title: Time
films.note: The documentary Sean thinks is available from Criterion.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: The Netflix/Criterion partnership Sean hopes Apple copies.

films.title: Spirited Away
films.note: A top-five family movie-night moment; the kids requested it again.

films.title: My Neighbor Totoro
films.note: The Ghibli entry point Sean hasn't bought the Steelbook of yet.

films.title: Kiki's Delivery Service
films.note: Gets a lot of play at Tim's Papa Movie Night.

films.title: The Boy and the Heron
films.note: The kids saw it dubbed; landed less cleanly than the linear Spirited Away.

films.title: Ponyo
films.note: Sean's joke DiCaprio interview subject and part of the Miyazaki thread.

films.title: What to Expect When You're Expecting
films.note: The premiere Tim cater-waited, where he once glimpsed DiCaprio.

The Third Part of the Night.title: The Third Part of the Night

One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest

What to Expect When You're Expecting.title: What to Expect When You're Expecting
